entries.author: Ethan
entries.body: T800 being used in robot fights in NYC — Eugene is the guy running it.

entries.author: Erik
entries.body: Robotics companies are increasingly adopting off-the-shelf batteries (DeWalt, etc.) rather than building custom power systems. Creates real tailwinds for industrial battery stocks. BorgWarner is one to watch.

entries.author: Ethan
entries.body: LITA is a hotel robotics rollup focused on operational efficiency. Currently evaluating a Miami hotel deployment. Interesting signal for physical AI adoption in hospitality.

entries.author: Raymond
entries.body: Tracking Automate 2026. Generalist robot demos were the dominant conversation — generalism is becoming the core narrative in industrial robotics, not task-specific hardware.

entries.author: Ethan
entries.body: Cardinal Robotics — a close contact closed a FANUC deal to finance their RaaS model. Robotics-as-a-service is getting real traction with institutional backing.

entries.author: Raymond
entries.body: Exploring ideas around micron-scale robot memory. Details incoming.

entries.author: Ethan
entries.body: Chinese manufacturing is structurally difficult for foreign companies to own. The winning move is to be structured as a Chinese entity with an American subsidiary as the public face. Several companies are doing this quietly.

entries.author: Ethan
entries.body: FedRAMP and cyber compliance for robots is an open problem nobody wants to solve themselves. Whoever builds the certification wrapper layer controls US market access for foreign hardware. Classic infrastructure play.
